rationale: Audience overlap is strong — the "woman's hand + relaxing puzzle app" hook indexes squarely on the 25-55 casual female puzzler we're targeting, and the satisfaction emotional pitch matches Marble Sort's calm/satisfying UI mood. However, there are two material frictions: (1) the jigsaw drag-and-drop mechanic actively misrepresents our core loop (tap-to-release physics + conveyor + color-sort), risking IPM dilution and bad post-install retention from mismatched intent; (2) the member palette (greys, blue, green) clashes with our lavender/pastel + orange accent identity. Recommend only as a test variant if reshot with marble-sort gameplay and our palette while preserving the "hand + phone + ASMR satisfaction" framing — do not license the centroid creative as-is.
